answer:
We need to count palindromes of length at least 2 that include the letter M, formed from the letters of MISSISSIPPI (M, I, I, I, I, S, S, S, S, P, P).  

A palindrome’s structure is determined by its first half; the second half mirrors it. For odd length, the middle letter can be any letter with an odd count. Since M appears only once, it can only be the middle letter in odd-length palindromes, or appear in both halves if used in an even-length palindrome (but then we’d need two Ms, which we don’t have). So M must be the center in any palindrome containing it.  

Counting all palindromes of length 2 or more with M as center, using remaining letters (I⁴, S⁴, P²) for the left half, yields 86 total.  

\boxed{C}
